steps.title: Identify the task
steps.content:
The student's goal is to "provide a specific example of an isthmus" — not define the term or describe geographic facts generally, but explicitly name a labeled example. The key word is specific: the answer must both label the thing as an isthmus and name it.
  • Choice A — There is a land bridge in Greece.: mentions a land bridge in Greece but names no specific isthmus — it is vague and provides no example. ✗
  • Choice B — In Greece, the Kassandra Peninsula is connected to mainland Greece.: states a geographic fact from the notes but never identifies anything as an isthmus or land bridge, so it is not an example of the concept. ✗
  • Choice C — One example of an isthmus is the Isthmus of Potidea in Greece.: explicitly labels the Isthmus of Potidea as "one example of an isthmus" and names it — directly accomplishing the goal. ✓
  • Choice D — An isthmus, also known as a land bridge, is a strip of land that connects two larger pieces of land across an expanse of water.: provides the definition of an isthmus, not an example of one. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Only C uses the phrasing "one example of an isthmus is..." followed by a specific named instance, which is precisely what the task requires. That is the correct answer.
